title: Object-Oriented Architecture and Capstone Delivery · Changed-Input Lab
summary: Deliver an extensible community-resource workflow with a domain core, replaceable boundaries, migrations, and operator evidence. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a production-shaped object-oriented capstone with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate domain rules, application orchestration, infrastructure adapters, and user interface concerns with explicit dependencies.
Use domain services only for cohesive rules that do not naturally belong to one entity or value object.
Version persisted representations and migrate data without coupling domain objects directly to historical storage schemas.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Add one tested, typed, inspectable program increment per activity.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Capstone Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend substitutability, invariants, typing, tests, persistence, failures, security, and maintainability with changed-case evidence.
